mechanism: NSAIDs inhibit platelet function and may cause GI bleeding; combined with warfarin bleeding risk is substantially increased
management: Avoid NSAIDs if possible; use acetaminophen alternative; monitor INR and signs of bleeding

mechanism: NSAIDs may reduce antihypertensive effect and impair renal function especially with ACE inhibitors
management: Monitor blood pressure and renal function; limit duration

mechanism: NSAIDs may increase risk of lactic acidosis in renal impairment via reduced renal perfusion
Low clinical significance for most patients
NSAIDs increase bleeding risk when combined with anticoagulants
